2603.25568.title: Are LLMs Overkill for Databases?: A Study on the Finiteness of SQL
2603.25568.paper_summary: Translating natural language to SQL for data retrieval has become more accessible thanks to code generation LLMs. But how hard is it to generate SQL code? While databases can become unbounded in complexity, the complexity of queries is bounded by real life utility and human needs. With a sample of 376 databases, we show that SQL queries, as translations of natural language questions are finite in practical complexity. There is no clear monotonic relationship between increases in database table count and increases in complexity of SQL queries. In their template forms, SQL queries follow a Power Law-like distribution of frequency where 70% of our tested queries can be covered with just 13% of all template types, indicating that the high majority of SQL queries are predictable. This suggests that while LLMs for code generation can be useful, in the domain of database access, they may be operating in a narrow, highly formulaic space where templates could be safer, cheaper, and auditable.
2603.25568.paper_summary_zh: 翻譯自然語言為 SQL 以進行數據檢索變得更加容易，這要歸功於代碼生成 LLM。那麼，生成 SQL 代碼有多難呢？雖然數據庫的複雜性可能無限，但查詢的複雜性受到現實生活實用性和人類需求的限制。通過對 376 個數據庫的樣本，我們顯示 SQL 查詢作為自然語言問題的翻譯在實際複雜性上是有限的。數據庫表的數量增加與 SQL 查詢的複雜性之間沒有明確的單調關係。在其模板形式中，SQL 查詢遵循類似於冪律的頻率分佈，其中 70% 的測試查詢僅用 13% 的所有模板類型即可涵蓋，這表明絕大多數 SQL 查詢是可預測的。這表明，雖然用於代碼生成的 LLM 可能有用，但在數據庫訪問領域，它們可能運行在一個狹窄且高度公式化的空間中，在這裡模板可能更安全、更便宜且可審計。
2603.25568.author: Yue Li et.al.
2603.25568.authors: Yue Li, David Mimno, Unso Eun Seo Jo
